topic: A person finds out their partner has been learning their native language
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the apartment felt suddenly vast, pressing in on me. I was making tea, the familiar clinking of the kettle a pathetic attempt to anchor myself to reality. Then, I overheard a snippet of conversation from the living room—a rapid-fire exchange in a language I hadn't heard spoken in years. My heart gave a hard, uneven thump against my ribs. I froze, the porcelain mug halfway to my lips. I slowly turned, my gaze fixed on him. His eyes met mine, and for a split second, I saw something sharp and unfamiliar in them—a knowledge that wasn't mine. My hands started to tremble, the ceramic warm and slick beneath my palms. I needed air, desperately, but my feet seemed rooted to the floor.

[story 2]

It happened while I was tidying up the study. A textbook, one I thought I’d put away months ago, lay open on the desk. The script scrolling across the page was intricate, beautiful, and undeniably my mother tongue. A cold wave washed over my skin, making the fine hairs on my arms stand up. I dropped the cloth I was holding; it landed with a soft thud that seemed deafening in the quiet room. I backed away slowly, my breath catching in my throat, tasting metallic and thin. I wanted to bolt, to disappear, but my legs felt weighted, as if submerged in thick mud. Every instinct screamed at me to run, yet I remained paralyzed, watching him across the room, a strange, unsettling awareness blooming in my chest.

[story 3]

I was scrolling through old photos on my phone when the realization hit me, sharp and sickening. It wasn't a misplaced word or a casual phrase; it was fluency. I replayed the audio clip in my head, the cadence, the inflection—it was perfect. A sudden, dizzying sensation spun through my vision. I dropped the phone onto the rug, the screen going dark. I stood rigid, every muscle tensed. I could feel the blood draining from my face, leaving a clammy chill. I took a shallow, ragged breath, trying to steady the frantic rhythm of my pulse. When he walked in, his casual greeting sounded like an accusation in the sudden, heavy quiet.
